eats.name: The Lobster Pot
eats.desc: Iconic waterfront since 1979 — lobster rolls, clam chowder, harbor views.

eats.name: The Mews Restaurant & Cafe
eats.desc: Oceanfront dining with a legendary martini list. A P-town institution.

eats.name: The Red Inn
eats.desc: Sunset dining right on the water. A splurge that earns it.

eats.name: The Canteen
eats.desc: Beachfront counter — go for the lobster roll and frozen rosé.

eats.name: Spiritus Pizza
eats.desc: Late-night Commercial Street slice. The after-bar ritual.

eats.name: Far Land Provisions
eats.desc: Build a beach picnic with their famous sandwiches.

eats.name: Truro Vineyards
eats.desc: Wine flights and live music under the trees. Food trucks on site.

eats.name: Blackfish
eats.desc: A former blacksmith shop turned buzzy bistro. Famous fish tacos.

eats.name: Montano's
eats.desc: Family Italian-American with a raw bar. Reliable Route 6 classic.

eats.name: Mac's Shack
eats.desc: Raw bar and sushi in a building topped by a boat. Oysters galore.

eats.name: Mac's Seafood
eats.desc: Order at the counter, eat on the harbor as the fleet comes in.
eats.deal: Thu: All-You-Can-Eat Fish & Chips · Daily Happy Hour

eats.name: PB Boulangerie Bistro
eats.desc: French-trained pastry. Croissants that ruin all other croissants.

eats.name: The Bookstore & Restaurant
eats.desc: Locals' joint over Duck Creek — oysters they pull themselves.

eats.name: The Beachcomber
eats.desc: Beach bar on the dunes at Cahoon Hollow. Live bands + raw bar.

eats.name: Winslow's Tavern
eats.desc: Porch dining in a sea-captain's house downtown. Brunch + dinner.

eats.name: Arnold's Lobster & Clam Bar
eats.desc: The standard for lobster rolls. Mini golf, ice cream, epic onion rings.

eats.name: PJ's
eats.desc: Fried seafood, lobster rolls & soft-serve — the Wellfleet favorite's new Eastham spot (former Friendly Fisherman).

eats.name: Hole in One Donuts
eats.desc: A Cape Sunday tradition — hand-cut donuts and a buzzing counter.

eats.name: Fairway Restaurant & Pizzeria
eats.desc: Diner breakfasts and pizza by the rail trail. Cyclists' refuel stop.

eats.name: The Knack
eats.desc: Modern clam-shack — smash burgers, lobster rolls, frappes.

eats.name: Hot Chocolate Sparrow
eats.desc: Coffee, cinnamon buns, and house chocolates. Orleans' morning HQ.

eats.name: Land Ho!
eats.desc: Quintessential Cape tavern since 1969 — chowder, burgers, ceiling signs.

eats.name: Mahoney's Atlantic Bar & Grill
eats.desc: Downtown bistro — wood-grilled fish and a lively bar scene.

eats.name: Brewster Fish House
eats.desc: Tiny, beloved, no-reservations — some of the best seafood on the Cape.

eats.name: Cobie's Clam Shack
eats.desc: Roadside classic on 6A. Fried clams and soft serve since 1948.

eats.name: JT's Seafood
eats.desc: Order-at-the-window family spot — fried seafood and lobster dinners.

eats.name: Chillingsworth
eats.desc: Special-occasion French prix-fixe in a 1689 estate. The big splurge.

eats.name: Snowy Owl Coffee Roasters
eats.desc: Craft roaster with a sunlit cafe. Best pour-over on the Mid-Cape.

eats.name: Cape Sea Grille
eats.desc: Refined coastal dining in a sea-captain's house. Quietly excellent.

eats.name: Brax Landing
eats.desc: Harbor-view deck — sunset cocktails and twin lobsters.

eats.name: Buca's Tuscan Roadhouse
eats.desc: Romantic, candle-lit Tuscan. Reservations a must in summer.

eats.name: The Impudent Oyster
eats.desc: Tucked off Main Street — bouillabaisse and oysters in a buzzy room.

eats.name: Chatham Pier Fish Market
eats.desc: Lobster rolls on the pier, watching seals and the fishing fleet.

eats.name: The Chatham Squire
eats.desc: Townie tavern and raw bar. Loud, fun, and always packed.

eats.name: Del Mar Bar & Bistro
eats.desc: Wood-fired Mediterranean with a sleek bar. Brunch is a scene.

eats.name: Hangar B Eatery
eats.desc: Breakfast on the airfield — watch small planes over your hash.

eats.name: Sesuit Harbor Café
eats.desc: BYOB sleeper hit — picnic tables right on the harbor. Cash only.

eats.name: Marshside
eats.desc: Water-view booths — Sunday brunch and big seafood plates.

eats.name: Sundae School
eats.desc: Homemade ice cream in an old schoolhouse. Don't skip the frozen pudding.

eats.name: Captain Frosty's
eats.desc: Fried-clam shack with soft serve. A 6A summer rite of passage.

eats.name: Scargo Cafe
eats.desc: Pre-theater dining across from the Cape Playhouse. Warm and reliable.

eats.name: Kream 'N Kone
eats.desc: Since 1953 — whole-belly clams and onion rings by the bucket.

eats.name: Captain Parker's Pub
eats.desc: Award-winning clam chowder. Marsh-view pub the locals swear by.
eats.deal: Specials Fri/Sat after 4pm + all day Sun

eats.name: The Optimist Cafe
eats.desc: Gingerbread Victorian — English tea, scones, and full breakfast.

eats.name: Inaho
eats.desc: Hidden-gem sushi behind a garden gate. Freshest fish on the Cape.

eats.name: Skipper Chowder House
eats.desc: Sea-view deck over Nantucket Sound. Chowder and big breakfasts.

eats.name: Four Seas Ice Cream
eats.desc: Since 1934 — the Cape's most legendary scoop. Get the peanut butter cup.

eats.name: Pain D'Avignon
eats.desc: Artisan bakery by day, candle-lit bistro by night. Worth the detour.

eats.name: Baxter's Boathouse
eats.desc: Fried clams on a dock deck as the boats slide by. Summer-only.

eats.name: Tugboats
eats.desc: Marina sunset spot — frozen drinks and lobster over the harbor.

eats.name: The Black Cat Tavern
eats.desc: Across from the ferries — chowder, raw bar, easy harbor views.

eats.name: Five Bays Bistro
eats.desc: Sleek village bistro — small plates and a buzzy bar crowd.

eats.name: Mattakeese Wharf
eats.desc: Harborside dining with live piano. Sunset over Barnstable Harbor.

eats.name: Barnstable Restaurant & Tavern
eats.desc: Village tavern in historic 6A — burgers, comfort food, and a patio.

eats.name: Bleu
eats.desc: Mashpee Commons brasserie — escargot, steak frites, lively bar.

eats.name: Siena
eats.desc: Tuscan trattoria at the Commons — handmade pasta, wood-grilled.

eats.name: Trevi Cafe & Wine Bar
eats.desc: Mediterranean small plates and a generous wine-by-the-glass list.

eats.name: Polar Cave Ice Cream
eats.desc: Cave-themed parlor with wild flavors. A kid (and adult) favorite.

eats.name: The Belfry Inn & Bistro
eats.desc: Dinner in a converted church — stained glass and refined plates.

eats.name: Seafood Sam's
eats.desc: Family fried-seafood institution by the canal. Order at the counter.

eats.name: The Dunbar Tea Room
eats.desc: Proper English tea and savory pies in a 1740s carriage house.

eats.name: Fishermen's View
eats.desc: Floor-to-ceiling marina views and their own seafood market downstairs.

eats.name: La Cucina Sul Mare
eats.desc: Main Street favorite — generous Italian, always a wait, always worth it.

eats.name: The Quarterdeck
eats.desc: Cozy Main Street standby — chowder, burgers, and a friendly bar.

eats.name: Añejo Mexican Bistro
eats.desc: Tequila bar and modern Mexican. Guac and margaritas on Main Street.

eats.name: The Captain Kidd
eats.desc: Waterfront tavern on Eel Pond — oysters and a historic back bar.

eats.name: Quicks Hole Taqueria
eats.desc: Fish tacos by the ferry dock. Quick, fresh, and fun.

eats.name: Maison Villatte
eats.desc: French bakers, line out the door — baguettes, tarts, croissants.

eats.name: The Chart Room
eats.desc: Summer-only harbor legend — mudslides and lobster on Red Brook Harbor.

eats.name: Stir Crazy
eats.desc: Beloved Southeast-Asian kitchen — curries and pho near the bridge.

eats.name: Mezza Luna
eats.desc: Buzzards Bay Italian-American since 1936. Sunday-gravy comfort.
eats.deal: Wed & Sat Prime Rib Special · weekday lunch specials

eats.name: The Brickhouse Restaurant
eats.desc: Fresh local seafood, Caribbean-inspired plates & hand-cut steaks on Route 6. Open 7 days.
eats.deal: Early Bird daily · Wed Prime Rib Night · Half-Off App Night
